records.ety: [AS. rust; akin to D. roest, G. & Sw. rost, Icel. ryð; -- named from its color, and akin to E. red. √113. See Red.]

defs: The reddish yellow coating formed on iron when exposed to moist air, consisting of ferric oxide or hydroxide; hence, by extension, any metallic film of corrosion.

A minute mold or fungus forming reddish or rusty spots on the leaves and stems of cereal and other grasses (Trichobasis Rubigo-vera), now usually believed to be a form or condition of the corn mildew (Puccinia graminis). As rust, it has solitary reddish spores; as corn mildew, the spores are double and blackish.
notes: ☞ Rust is also applied to many other minute fungi which infest vegetation, such as the species of Ustilago, Uredo, and Lecythea.

defs: That which resembles rust in appearance or effects.
A composition used in making a rust joint. See Rust joint, below.
Foul matter arising from degeneration; as, rust on salted meat.
Corrosive or injurious accretion or influence.
quotes: Sacred truths cleared from all rust and dross of human mixtures.
notes: ☞ Rust is used in the formation of compounds of obvious meaning; as, rust-colored, rust-consumed, rust-eaten, and the like.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rusted; p. pr. & vb. n. Rusting.]

defs: To contract rust; to be or become oxidized.
quotes: If gold ruste, what shall iron do?

defs: To be affected with the parasitic fungus called rust; also, to acquire a rusty appearance, as plants.
To degenerate in idleness; to become dull or impaired by inaction.
quotes: Must I rust in Egypt? never more Appear in arms, and be the chief of Greece?

defs: To cause to contract rust; to corrode with rust; to affect with rust of any kind.
quotes: Keep up your bright swords, for the dew will rust them.

defs: To impair by time and inactivity.
